# ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0020 - QUIC RFC 9000 Long-Header ACK Piggyback Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the bounded Initial and Handshake packet-composition floor that prepends pending packet-space ACK frames to live CRYPTO packets without widening retry, replay, interop, or public API claims.

## Scope

This design covers pending ACK prefixing for live Initial and Handshake CRYPTO packet flushes only. It does not claim retry, retried Initial replay, CRYPTO retransmission, candidate-path or multipath scheduling, endpoint interop success, public API behavior, or a general delayed-ACK timer.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010

## Design Summary

The long-header CRYPTO packet builders accept transient prefix frame bytes before the CRYPTO frame payload. The runtime Initial and Handshake flush paths check ACK-generation state for the matching packet number space before protecting live CRYPTO packets. When an ACK frame is due, the runtime formats only the ACK frame bytes, passes them as the packet prefix, protects ACK plus CRYPTO in one Initial or Handshake packet, tracks the sent packet under the actual packet number, and records the ACK frame as sent for that packet-space trigger. If no ACK is pending, the builders and runtime preserve the existing CRYPTO-only packet layout.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicHandshakeFlowCoordinator.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.Protocol.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/QuicS13AckPiggybackTestSupport.cs
- benchmarks/QuicHandshakePacketProtectionBenchmarks.cs

## Data and State Considerations

ACK prefix bytes are transient packet-composition data. Initial and Handshake sent-packet tracking remains packet-number-space specific, and ACK-generation state records the largest ack-eliciting receipt covered by the piggybacked ACK frame so duplicate ACK-only or repeated piggyback triggers are suppressed for the same receipt.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Only live Initial and Handshake CRYPTO flush paths are in scope.
- Retry bootstrap replay, retried Initial replay, and CRYPTO retransmission builders continue to emit their existing CRYPTO payloads without ACK prefixing.
- No ACK frame is invented when the relevant Initial or Handshake ACK-generation state has no pending ack-eliciting receipt.
- ACK prefixing does not alter CRYPTO stream offsets or CRYPTO frame bytes.
- Application Data stream sends and NEW_TOKEN topoffs remain owned by ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0018 and ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0019.

## Alternatives Considered

- Widen ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0018 to cover all packet spaces; rejected because that slice intentionally proved only active-path 1-RTT Application Data stream sends.
- Treat the server NEW_TOKEN topoff as proof for Initial and Handshake packets; rejected because NEW_TOKEN uses the Application Data packet builder, not the long-header CRYPTO builders.
- Prefix retransmitted CRYPTO packets as well; rejected for this slice because retransmission ownership needs separate proof that ACK frames are not replayed as retransmittable payload.
- Classify this as interop success; rejected because this slice proves local packet composition only.

## Risks

- General delayed-ACK timer behavior remains unclaimed.
- Retry/replay/retransmission ACK piggyback behavior remains unclaimed until separately traced.
- Endpoint interop success and public API behavior remain outside this architecture slice.
